description: Create a shell consisting of a collection of shell finite elements. The five main keywords articulate five creation techniques: (1) create a collection of elements from a set of block faces ( by-block-face ); (2) create a single element from three existing nodes ( by-nodeids ); (3) create a collection of elements from a quadrilateral shape ( by-quadrilateral ); (4) create a collection of elements from a triangular shape ( by-triangle ); and (5) create a collection of elements from a set of zone faces ( by-zone-face ). For technique 2, newly created nodes will remain unattached. If you wish to attach them to a target object, position them with the structure node initialize position command. A shell can also be created by the structure shell import command.

6.0.description: Create a shell. The four main keywords articulate four different techniques of creation: by creating a collection of elements from a group of zone faces (by-face); by creating a single element from four existing nodes (by-nodes); from a specified quadrilateral (by-quadrilateral); or from a triangle (by-triangle).

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: Specify the ID that will be assigned to all new elements created by this command. If this value is not given, then the next available ID will be used. The ID controls potential joining of the element nodes to an existing element, as follows. At each potential new node location, a new node is created unless all of the following conditions are fulfilled: 1) the ID is given; 2) an existing node is found “near” the potential new node location; and 3) the found node is part of an element of the same type with ID equal to i.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the created elements to group s1. The group is assigned to the slot named s2 if supplied; it is assigned to the slot named Default if not. Use of the group logic is described in Group.

keywords.name: cross-diagonal
keywords.description: If specified, a cross-diagonal mesh of four triangles will be created with a node lying at the centroid of all quadrilaterals; otherwise, a cross-hatch mesh of two triangles will be created dividing the quadrilateral.

keywords.name: distinct
keywords.description: Eliminate sharing of nodes among created elements. Every element will create a new set of nodes, even if they are at the same location in space and elements share the same ID number. This option is most commonly used with a later structure node join command to create links connecting nodes at the same location in order to apply custom inter-element behavior.

keywords.name: element-type-s
keywords.description: Attempt to match the string s to one of the available keywords below, and treats as that option.

keywords.name: element-type-cst
keywords.description: CST (6 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a membrane element; it resists membrane, but not bending, loading.

keywords.name: element-type-csth
keywords.description: CST Hybrid (9 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a membrane element; it resists membrane, but not bending, loading.

keywords.name: element-type-dkt
keywords.description: DKT (9 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a plate-bending element; it resists bending, but not membrane, loading.

keywords.name: element-type-dkt-cst
keywords.description: DKT-CST (15 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a shell element that combines the DKT and CST elements to resist both bending and membrane loading.

keywords.name: element-type-dkt-csth
keywords.description: DKT-(CST Hybrid) (18 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a shell element that combines the DKT and CST Hybrid elements to resist both bending and membrane loading.

keywords.name: internal
keywords.description: This keyword only applies if the from-faces option was used. Specifies that all internal and surface faces in the range will have elements created on them. Normally only surface faces are considered.

keywords.name: maximum-length
keywords.description: Break the element into segments no greater than length f. The segments setting, if provided, is overridden as needed by this setting.

keywords.name: segments
keywords.description: Break the element into i equal segments, creating new nodes as needed. The default value is 1, indicating that only one element will be created.

keywords.name: size
keywords.description: This keyword only applies if the from-quadrilateral option was used. Increases the number of elements created by subdividing the quadrilateral into i1 segments along the first edge and i2 segments along the second. By default, only one quad is created.

7.0.description: Create a shell. The four main keywords articulate four different techniques of creation: by creating a collection of elements from a group of zone faces (by-face); by creating a single element from four existing nodes (by-nodes); from a specified quadrilateral (by-quadrilateral); or from a triangle (by-triangle).

keywords.name: by-quadrilateral
keywords.description: Create elements based on four points forming a quadrilateral, in order. The element local system will point in the direction determined by using the right-hand rule.

keywords.name: by-triangle
keywords.description: Create elements based on three points forming a triangle. The element local system will point in the direction determined by using the right-hand rule.

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: Specify the ID that will be assigned to all new elements created by this command. If this value is not given, then the next available ID will be used. The ID controls potential joining of the element nodes to an existing element, as follows. At each potential new node location, a new node is created unless all of the following conditions are fulfilled: 1) the ID is given; 2) an existing node is found “near” the potential new node location; and 3) the found node is part of an element of the same type with ID equal to i.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the created elements to group s1. The group is assigned to the slot named s2 if supplied; it is assigned to the slot named Default if not. Use of the group logic is described in Groups.

keywords.name: cross-diagonal
keywords.description: If specified, a cross-diagonal mesh of four triangles will be created with a node lying at the centroid of all quadrilaterals; otherwise, a cross-hatch mesh of two triangles will be created dividing the quadrilateral.

keywords.name: distinct
keywords.description: Eliminate sharing of nodes among created elements. Every element will create a new set of nodes, even if they are at the same location in space and elements share the same ID number. This option is most commonly used with a later structure node join command to create links connecting nodes at the same location in order to apply custom inter-element behavior.

keywords.name: element-type-adummy
keywords.description: Attempt to match the string s to one of the available keywords below, and treats as that option.

keywords.name: element-type-cst
keywords.description: CST (6 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a membrane element; it resists membrane, but not bending, loading.

keywords.name: element-type-csth
keywords.description: CST Hybrid (9 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a membrane element; it resists membrane, but not bending, loading.

keywords.name: element-type-dkt
keywords.description: DKT (9 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a plate-bending element; it resists bending, but not membrane, loading.

keywords.name: element-type-dkt-cst
keywords.description: DKT-CST (15 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a shell element that combines the DKT and CST elements to resist both bending and membrane loading.

keywords.name: element-type-dkt-csth
keywords.description: DKT-(CST Hybrid) (18 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a shell element that combines the DKT and CST Hybrid elements to resist both bending and membrane loading.

keywords.name: internal
keywords.description: This keyword only applies if the from-faces option was used. Specifies that all internal and surface faces in the range will have elements created on them. Normally only surface faces are considered.

keywords.name: maximum-length
keywords.description: Break the element into segments no greater than length f. The segments setting, if provided, is overridden as needed by this setting.

keywords.name: reverse
keywords.description: This keyword only applies if the from-faces option was used. Specifies that the orientation of the element will be reversed from the default with respect to the face, so that the element local Z direction faces the other direction. Also if the cross-diagonal keyword was not used the diagonal will appear in the other direction with respect to quadrilateral faces.

keywords.name: segments
keywords.description: Break the element into i equal segments, creating new nodes as needed. The default value is 1, indicating that only one element will be created.

keywords.name: size
keywords.description: This keyword only applies if the from-quadrilateral option was used. Increases the number of elements created by subdividing the quadrilateral into i1 segments along the first edge and i2 segments along the second. By default, only one quad is created.

keywords.name: by-block-face
keywords.description: Create new elements that are attached to the set of 3-sided 3DEC zone faces in the range. Elements can only be created on block surfaces. New nodes associated with each element will also be created. The nodes of each element will be ordered counter-clockwise with respect to the outside of the zone faces, thereby making each element \(z\) axis point outward. The orientation of the \(z\) axis can be reversed with the reverse keyword.

keywords.name: by-nodeids
keywords.description: Create a single element from three existing nodes.

keywords.name: by-quadrilateral
keywords.description: Create elements based on four points forming a quadrilateral, in order. The element \(z\) -axis will point in the direction determined by applying the right-hand rule to the quadrilateral.

keywords.name: by-triangle
keywords.description: Create elements based on three points forming a triangle. The element \(z\) -axis will point in the direction determined by applying the right-hand rule to the triangle.

keywords.name: by-zone-face
keywords.description: Create new elements that are attached to the set of three- or four-sided zone-faces in the range. By default, only surface faces will be considered, and the internal keyword can be used to select internal faces. New nodes associated with each element will also be created. The nodes of each element will be ordered counter-clockwise with respect to the outside of the zone faces, thereby making each element \(z\) axis point outward. The orientation of the \(z\) axis can be reversed with the reverse keyword. Note that after creating the elements with this command, the zones may be deleted and the elements may be positioned by moving their nodes with the structure node initialize position command.

keywords.name: cross-diagonal
keywords.description: If specified, a cross-diagonal mesh of four triangles will be created with a node lying at the centroid of all quadrilaterals; otherwise, a cross-hatch mesh of two triangles will be created dividing the quadrilateral. If this keyword is not specified, then a cross-hatch mesh will be created. [ 1 ]

keywords.name: distinct
keywords.description: Eliminate sharing of nodes among created elements. Every element will create a new set of nodes, even if they are at the same location in space and elements share the same ID number. This option is most commonly used with a later structure node join command to create links connecting nodes at the same location in order to apply custom inter-element behavior.

keywords.name: dkt-csth
keywords.description: Specify the type of finite element to be used by each structural element. A description of the available shell finite elements is provided here . If no element type is specified, then the element type will be DKT-CST. [ 2 ] Note that the type of finite element is associated with each structural element when it is created and cannot subsequently be altered. Plastic material behavior is supported by the DKT, CST and DKT-CST finite elements. The keyword must be one of the following entries: Attempt to match the string s to one of the available keywords below, and treat as that option. CST (6 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a membrane element; it resists membrane, but not bending, loading. CST Hybrid (9 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a membrane element; it resists membrane, but not bending, loading. DKT (9 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a plate-bending element; it resists bending, but not membrane, loading. DKT-CST (15 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a shell element that combines the DKT and CST elements to resist both bending and membrane loading. DKT-(CST Hybrid) (18 degrees-of-freedom) finite element. This is a shell element that combines the DKT and CST Hybrid elements to resist both bending and membrane loading.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the created elements to group s1 . The group is assigned to the slot named s2 if supplied; it is assigned to the slot named Default if not. Use of the group logic is described in Groups .

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: Specify the ID (this is the collection ID) that will be assigned to all new elements created by this command. If this value is not given, then the next available ID will be used. The ID controls potential joining of the element nodes to an existing element, as follows. At each potential new node location, a new node is created unless all of the following conditions are fulfilled: 1) the ID is given; 2) an existing node is found “near” the potential new node location; and 3) the found node is part of an element of the same type with ID equal to i .

keywords.name: internal
keywords.description: This keyword only applies if the by-zone-face option was used. Specifies that all internal and surface faces in the range will have elements created on them. Normally only surface faces are considered.

keywords.name: maximum-length
keywords.description: Break the element into segments no greater than length f . The segments setting, if provided, is overridden as needed by this setting.

keywords.name: reverse
keywords.description: This keyword only applies if the by-zone-faces option was used. Specifies that the orientation of the element will be reversed from the default with respect to the face, so that the element \(z\) axis points in the other direction. Also, if the cross-diagonal keyword was not used, then the diagonal will appear in the other direction with respect to quadrilateral faces.

keywords.name: segments
keywords.description: Break the element into i equal segments, creating new nodes as needed. The default value is 1 , indicating that only one element will be created.

keywords.name: size
keywords.description: This keyword only applies if the by-quadrilateral option was used. Increases the number of elements created by subdividing the quadrilateral into i1 segments along the first edge and i2 segments along the second. By default, only one quad is created.
